page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz and includes an 8 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, it bundles a 150 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, while network connectivity is provisioned with a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates varied performance characteristics for this hardware profile. In CPU-intensive tasks, the instance registers average performance in single-threaded gzip compression, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-threaded operations show limitations, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falling into the bottom 10% of benchmarks. Cryptographic capabilities remain moderate, as evidenced by an average rating in the PassMark CPU encryption test, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instruction sets.

Memory and storage performance present distinct tradeoffs. The DDR4 memory subsystem exhibits weak to poor throughput, with PassMark memory scores in the bottom 10% and uncached read/write bandwidths in the bottom 25%. Despite these memory constraints, the integrated 150 GB NVMe SSD provides fast local storage access. This combination makes the instance suitable for workloads that require moderate compute and local scratch space, such as single-threaded text processing, basic web serving, and lightweight database operations, while it is less suited for memory-bound or highly parallelized multi-core computing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 150 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results reveal average performance in single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and Geekbench single-core tests, though multi-threaded CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the lower percentiles. The inclusion of dedicated local NVMe storage offsets memory throughput limitations for specific workloads. This instance is suited for applications requiring moderate compute capacity and fast local scratch space, such as lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 150 GB local NVMe SSD for fast scratch storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.xlarge features 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 150 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for moderate compute workloads.
tagline: An x86 compute instance combining dedicated AMD EPYC processors with fast local NVMe storage for balanced single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.3 GHz AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
Includes 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 150 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Delivers average performance in single-threaded processing and cryptographic tasks
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic database operations
